text: This is the case of the thymectomy.
The surgeon initiates dissection between the adipose tissue and the pericardium, carefully tracing the path of the phrenic nerve upwards.
The surgeon uses fenestrated forceps and a bipolar Maryland dissector to retract and dissect along the tissue plane, following the anatomical structures.
The surgeon demonstrates the wristed articulation of the instruments, allowing precise orientation during the thymectomy procedure.
The movements required by the surgeon are actually quite subtle.
The innominate vein, rhema vein, and superior vena cava (SVC) are clearly visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon uses wristed instruments to perform subtle sweeping motions, effectively dissecting the fat located between the innominate and rhema veins.
